title: Resident Technical Officer (MEP)
description:
Responsibilities

  • To perform all roles and responsibilities required of site supervisors under the Building Control Act.
  • To supervise the timely execution of the works and maintain quality control and ensure that the works are carried out in accordance with the contract specifications, drawings, allocated timeframe, all other contract documents and site instructions.
  • To check all drawings and specifications to ensure that works are carried out in accordance with the approved plans for discrepancies and report to the Superintending Officer / Consultant / Qualified Person as stated below:
  1. Check shop drawings submitted by contractor.
  2. Check as-built drawings and Operation & Maintenance Manual submitted by contractor.
  • To check on quality of materials and samples delivered to site and report to Superintending Officer / Consultant / Qualified Person on any materials that are not conforming to the specifications.
  • To check mock-ups done on site, where necessary.
  • To ensure that the required construction tests in connection with the construction works are carried out where necessary.
  • To prepare defect lists, monitor progress of rectification works and ensure all defects are satisfactorily rectified.
  • Write daily reports of works done by contractor and record daily manpower status on site.
  • Inspect and maintain records of equipment/machinery/licensed machinery operator/permits (both statutory and aviation requirements).
  • To ensure that the structural elements or critical works of the construction works are being carried out in accordance with the approved plans and with any terms and conditions imposed by the Commissioner of Building Control where necessary.

Qualifications

  • Diploma in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering from a Polytechnic or equivalent.
  • Minimum 8 years of relevant experience as a RTO, of which at least 5 years shall be in supervision of works similar in nature to major construction works in buildings, services laying and lighting & control systems.
  • Accreditation with the Joint Accreditation Committee of the Institution of Engineers Singapore and the Association of Consulting Engineers Singapore are preferred.
  • Certificate in Building Construction Supervisors Safety (BCSS) Course.

postedCompany.description:
ABOUT WSP
As one of the world's leading professional services firms, WSP provides technical expertise and strategic advice to clients in the Property & Buildings, Transportation & Infrastructure, Environment, Industry, Resources (including Mining and Oil & Gas) and Power & Energy sectors. We also offer highly specialized services in project delivery and strategic consulting. Our experts include engineers, advisors, technicians, scientists, architects, planners, surveyors and environmental specialists, as well as other design, program and construction management professionals. With approximately 36,000 talented people in 500 offices across 40 countries, we are uniquely positioned to deliver successful and sustainable projects, wherever our clients need us. www.wsp.com.
